question.text: Under Section 15C of the SEBI Act, what is the penalty for failure to redress investors' grievances after being directed by SEBI?

answer.text: Not less than ten lakh rupees, up to twenty-five crore rupees
Not less than five lakh rupees, up to ten crore rupees
A flat penalty of two lakh rupees
Not less than one lakh rupees per day, up to a maximum of one crore rupees

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Section 15C prescribes a minimum penalty of not less than one lakh rupees which may extend to one lakh rupees per day during which the failure continues, with a maximum of one crore rupees. **The Trap:** Higher penalty amounts are reserved for more serious violations like insider trading (Section 15G). **Cross-Exam Context:** Investor grievance redressal penalties are tested in NISM Series III-A and V-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When SEBI issues a written direction to redress grievances, treat it as a top priority - daily penalties start from the deadline date.
